IDF Analyzer

Tool Guide

Back to App
Open Input Views Analysis Summary Metrics Edit epJSON Version Developer Build Update Log

Open and Analyze

Open supports `.idf`, `.imf`, `.epjson`, `.json`, and `.txt` files. Analyze detects IDF or epJSON from the file extension and content. The startup sample is the official EnergyPlus `RefBldgLargeOfficeNew2004_Chicago.idf` example from NREL/EnergyPlus.

Input Views

Text

A fully expanded editable object summary first, with raw IDF or epJSON text below it and optional position sync from editable fields and analysis selections.

JSON

A structured epJSON-like editor with read-only syntax, inline-editable value tokens, search, collapse depth, object focus, and the shared raw text pane below.

Table

Fully expanded object type tables with fixed row headers, switchable objects-as-rows or fields-as-rows layout, and shared raw text position sync.

Resizable Split

The input and analysis panels have separate scroll areas, a locked app window, draggable left/right divider, and draggable vertical dividers for Raw Text and Geometry details.

Analysis Views

Summary

The right panel currently shows one Summary view with categorized model, geometry, envelope, load, schedule, and HVAC metrics.

Geometry

Geometry parses detailed zones, walls, roofs, floors, and fenestration into a 3D view that defaults to all levels, optional story filtering, a story plan view, selectable properties, and Sync locate jumps to the matching input object.

Export

Summary exports are available as categorized JSON or a two-column CSV with `name,value` rows.

Summary Metric Catalog

The metric guide below is loaded from the same backend catalog used by analysis and export. Each item documents its source, method, assumptions, and missing-data behavior.

Loading summary metric guide

Edit, Convert, and Export

Remove unused currently targets schedule, material, construction, curve, and table objects that are not referenced by other fields. To IDF, To epJSON, and Remove unused are grouped under Tools. Text summary, Table, and inline JSON value edits patch the backend model or field text and regenerate the source text, epJSON preview, tables, and analysis report. Download exports the current editor text. Export JSON and Export CSV in the Summary panel export the current model summary.

epJSON Shape Notes

EnergyPlus epJSON is schema-driven. The current converter preserves object groups and now emits detailed surface and shading vertices as a `vertices` array of coordinate objects. Broader IDD/schema-aware typing and extensible-field handling remains an implementation target.

Version and IDD References

When a Version object is present, EnergyPlus version 22 or newer is accepted. Future version-specific IDD and epJSON schema validation can be added under `resources/energyplus/<major>.<minor>/`.

Developer Build

Use `dev.bat` from the repository root to run setup, tests, builds, and verification through PowerShell with the required execution-policy bypass flags. 

.\dev.bat setup
.\dev.bat test
.\dev.bat build
.\dev.bat verify

Update Log

  • 2026-05-29 Made Geometry 3D default to all levels, changed locate into a Sync locate checkbox, and synced Raw Text position for right-panel object jumps when enabled.
  • 2026-05-29 Switched the startup model to the official EnergyPlus large office reference IDF and added vertical splitters for Raw Text and Geometry details.
  • 2026-05-29 Added a Geometry result tab with Three.js 3D massing, story plan view, zone/wall/window toggles, and selected-object property details.
  • 2026-05-29 Rebuilt the right panel as a single Summary metric catalog with categorized metrics, JSON/CSV export, and backend-sourced metric guide entries.
  • 2026-05-29 Removed Advanced epJSON text, moved Raw Text into a shared pane for all input views, avoided synthetic Name columns in Table/Text, and added schema-aware `vertices` arrays for detailed surface epJSON output.
  • 2026-05-29 Made Text view summary fields editable and added optional action-based position sync between the formatted summary and raw text.
  • 2026-05-29 Locked window-level app scrolling so only panels scroll, and changed JSON value editing from a popup to inline token editing.
  • 2026-05-29 Rebuilt JSON view as a structured editor with read-only syntax tokens, inline-editable value tokens, search, collapse depth, backend model patching, and raw text editing.
  • 2026-05-29 Split the frontend script into ES modules under `frontend/dist/js/` and consolidated agent notes into `docs/agent.md`.
  • 2026-05-29 Reorganized the toolbar, removed the Objects result tab, added analysis-to-input object jumps, and expanded zone details with surface and related-object lists.
  • 2026-05-29 Restyled JSON structured view as an epJSON-shaped tree with quoted keys, braces, colons, and literal values.
  • 2026-05-29 Flattened the JSON structured view so IDF objects start at the top of the tree and use smaller indentation.
  • 2026-05-29 Improved input view readability with semantic JSON tree labels, shallower indentation, fully expanded Text/Table groups, and steadier table row headers.
  • 2026-05-29 Reworked Text and JSON view hierarchy, added table transpose controls, and added the resizable input-analysis splitter.
  • 2026-05-29 Replaced the starter sample with a larger multi-zone model and changed Table view to object-type tables.
  • 2026-05-29 Added Text, JSON, and Table input views with cached structured model rendering.
  • 2026-05-29 Guide navigation now stays inside the Wails WebView to avoid external `wails.localhost` connection failures.
  • 2026-05-29 Added IDF/epJSON auto-detection, EnergyPlus 22+ version detection, and conversion workflow.
  • 2026-05-29 Initial guide for open, analysis, edit, and developer build workflow.
